image: epithelioid cell type and prominent nucleoli (arrow).jpg
res:
Microscopic findings: ---
Pathologic diagnosis:
Conjunctiva, OD, excisional biopsy
---Primary acquired melanosis with severe atypia

Microscopic findings:
Sections show tissue with primary acquired melanosis with severe atypia, composed of proliferation of melanocytic cells in radial and vertical phase. Isolated nevus cell nest is noted. Slightly enlarged of melanocytic nuclei is identified focally. These melanocytes are positive for melan-A, BRAF, and HMB45. Ki67 labeling index is low (approximately 5%)
==
C-MIN score: 5 
Radial spread: 3   
Vertical spread: 1  
Melanocytic atypia: 1
==
Proposed WHO grading system: High grade C-MIL
gts:
Microscopic findings: Epithelioid melanoma cells with prominent nucleoli are present.
Pathological diagnosis: Choroidal melanoma
